  2. Overnight Runs

Running Ralph Overnight

Set it, forget it, wake up to committed code. Here's how to do it safely.

01

Safety First

Important considerations before running unattended

Ralph is Safe by Design

Ralph always creates Git commits before making changes. This means you can always see exactly what changed and revert if needed. However, running any automated tool overnight requires some preparation.

Always Commit First

Before running Ralph overnight, commit any uncommitted changes. This gives you a clean baseline to compare against.

Set Iteration Limits

Use Standard (-S) or Thorough (-T) modes. Avoid Long (-L) for overnight runs unless you want maximum iterations.

Monitor API Costs

Check your AI provider's dashboard for spending limits. Set alerts for unexpected usage spikes.

Review PROMPT.md

Double-check your spec for clarity. Vague specs can lead to unexpected results when you're not watching.

02

Recommended Configuration

Settings optimized for overnight runs

toml ~/.ralph/config.toml 
# Overnight run configuration

agent = "claude-code"
fallback = ["codex", "opencode"]

[reviewer]
model = "claude-sonnet-4"
max_iterations = 10  # Reasonable limit for overnight

[timeout]
# Stop after 2 hours max (in seconds)
max_duration = 7200

[logging]
# Save detailed logs for review
level = "info"
file = "ralph-overnight.log"

Configuration Tips

max_iterations
Set a reasonable cap. 10 iterations is usually enough for complex tasks without runaway loops.
max_duration
Safety timeout in seconds. 7200 (2 hours) is generous for most tasks.
logging.file
Save logs to a file so you can review what happened while you slept.
fallback
If your primary agent fails, Ralph can try alternatives. Useful for long-running tasks.
03

Running Overnight

Step-by-step process for unattended runs

1

Prepare Your Workspace

bash 
# Make sure you're on a clean branch
git status

# Commit any pending changes
git add . && git commit -m "Pre-overnight checkpoint"

# Optional: create a new branch for the overnight work
git checkout -b feature/overnight-task
2

Write a Clear PROMPT.md

Make sure your spec is detailed and unambiguous. Include constraints to prevent scope creep.

3

Start Ralph with Logging

bash 
# Run in Thorough mode with output to log file
ralph -T 2>&1 | tee ralph-overnight.log

# Or use nohup to keep it running after you close the terminal
nohup ralph -T > ralph-overnight.log 2>&1 &
4

Go to Sleep

Ralph will iterate until the code is approved or the iteration limit is reached. Sweet dreams.

04

Morning Review

What to check when you wake up

Check the Log

# See how Ralph did
cat ralph-overnight.log

# Or just the end
tail -50 ralph-overnight.log

Review Git History

# See what commits Ralph made
git log --oneline -10

# See what changed
git diff HEAD~1

Test the Changes

Run your test suite. Try the new feature manually. Make sure everything works as expected.

Revert if Needed

# If something went wrong, revert to before
git reset --hard HEAD~1

# Or go back to your checkpoint
git checkout main
05

Cost Control

Keep your API bills under control

Set Spending Limits

Most AI providers let you set monthly spending caps. Configure these before running overnight to avoid surprises.

  • Anthropic: Settings → Usage Limits
  • OpenAI: Settings → Billing → Usage Limits

Estimate Costs

A typical Ralph run with 10 iterations might cost:

  • Simple task: $0.50 - $2.00
  • Medium task: $2.00 - $5.00
  • Complex task: $5.00 - $15.00

Actual costs depend on your AI provider, model, and code complexity.

Use Efficient Models

For overnight runs on simpler tasks, consider using faster, cheaper models. Configure in your config.toml.

Ready to automate your night?

Write your spec, configure your timeouts, and let Ralph work while you sleep.

Write Your Spec → See Workflow Examples

Ralph Workflow

Multi-agent AI orchestration for automated code development. Ship your ideas with autonomous agents that collaborate until quality is achieved.
